title: A little about me
text:
Hi, I'm Nazar. Here's what I believe about branding, and why I do it this way.

A brand isn't a logo — it's an asset. I've seen it work both ways: the businesses that grow fastest aren't the ones with the prettiest visuals, they're the ones people remember, trust, and are willing to pay more for. That's not decoration. That's equity, and I build it the same way any real asset gets built — deliberately, and over time.

So I don't start with design. I start with the business — what makes it different, what position it can actually own in a crowded market, what a customer needs to feel to choose it over the five other options next door. Only then do I move into the visual identity — logo, color, type, voice — built to carry that position, not just to look good on its own.

That's the standard I hold my own work to: not "does this look nice," but "does this make the business worth more, longer."
